client.captureScope: installed application bundle and packaged renderer resources

sources.scope: version, build, bundle id and Chromium version
installed bundle identity and notarized signature
packaged renderer files and stable selector presence
semantic role mapping and paint-only transparent-surface rules
live app shell, sidebar, primary route and post-injection ownership markers

surfaceTargets.policy: transparent structural viewport above context-specific main scrim
remove only the native opaque gradient so one window artwork remains continuous
paint one bounded semantic surface without repainting the artwork or resize edge
clear the layout root and retain one translucent readable input surface
moving backgrounds change paint only; optional static cards layout remains separately guarded
semantic Settings surfaces stay readable without a second wallpaper or boundary shadow; native pages remain unchanged

insert one Engine-owned CC Theme navigation item immediately after Appearance and render a dedicated right-side page; derive geometry, typography, paint, state and motion from the adjacent live native navigation item instead of hardcoding a client release; preserve independent ownership and keyboard semantics; edit only the current external theme through collapsed paint-token groups; restore native content on exit and never copy native event handlers, mutate Appearance controls, add a toolbar action or change host geometry
map native writing-block variables to translucent semantic colors
preserve native terminal geometry while mapping panel and xterm paint tokens
keep the native browser portal above the themed application mount

styleChanges: static artwork is painted once at the window root, including Settings
Home and task/library contexts use separate scrim strength without layout changes
moving-background Home roles receive paint-only card and label treatment
migrationFromPreviousCatalog.strategy: prefer packaged stable attributes, translate them to Theme Surface Roles, and isolate version-sensitive fallbacks in the adapter

limitations: The active development task remained in this Codex process, so validation reused its verified loopback CDP session instead of restarting the application.
The live shell and post-injection node contract passed; the lazy CC Theme navigation and dedicated page are rechecked whenever Settings is mounted.
Hashed asset file names are evidence references only and are never used as runtime selectors.
Future client builds still require a new compatibility profile before release.
